title: Stacked Changes, Maintainer Workflows, and Integration Queues · Mastery Check
summary: A maintainer review compares near-miss operations for stacked changes, maintainer workflows, and integration queues and requires the smallest safe stacked-change and maintainer integration protocol with topology and content evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for stacked-change and maintainer integration protocol. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Represent dependent changes as explicit base and tip relationships, keep each review independently testable, and update downstream stacks after revision.
Use range-diff and interdiff-style evidence to explain rerolls, reordered commits, changed commit messages, and semantic deltas between series.
Compare topic-branch, integration-branch, subsystem, patch-flow, merge-queue, and release-train workflows by authority and recovery cost.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Gita Stacked Change Dependencies · changed case
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Case G22 is a quiz in a disposable repository. the reroll changes only the requested validation patch and preserves stack dependencies. The learner must predict old and new series map by patch identity with reordered and amended entries visible, run git range-diff main...stack-v1 main...stack-v2, explain one patch changed, two map exactly, and the dependent tip moved to a new parent, test this changed condition: reorder the dependency after its consumer and reject the stack despite an equal final tree, and preserve this recovery boundary: retain both series refs and review comments until the integrated exact revision is published.

Which action demonstrates Gita Stacked Change Dependencies without dropping earlier contracts?
